tips.title: Right-size underutilized Virtual Machines
tips.description: Azure Advisor identifies VMs with average CPU below 5% or network utilization below 7 Mbps over 7 days. Switch to a smaller VM size or deallocate idle VMs to reduce compute costs.

tips.title: Purchase Reserved Instances for steady workloads
tips.description: Azure Reserved VM Instances provide up to 72% discount over pay-as-you-go pricing with 1-year or 3-year commitments. Best for production workloads with predictable usage patterns.

tips.title: Use Azure Savings Plans for flexible compute
tips.description: Azure Savings Plans for Compute offer up to 65% savings with hourly spend commitment that applies across VM series, regions, and operating systems. More flexible than Reserved Instances.

tips.title: Use Spot VMs for interruptible workloads
tips.description: Azure Spot VMs offer up to 90% discount for workloads that can tolerate interruption — batch processing, dev/test, CI/CD pipelines, and stateless microservices.

tips.title: Apply Azure Hybrid Benefit for Windows and SQL
tips.description: Bring existing Windows Server and SQL Server licenses with Software Assurance to Azure. Saves up to 85% on Windows VMs and up to 55% on Azure SQL compared to pay-as-you-go.

tips.title: Auto-shutdown non-production VMs
tips.description: Configure auto-shutdown schedules for dev/test/staging VMs during off-hours and weekends. Use Azure Automation or VM auto-shutdown feature to stop VMs when not needed.

tips.title: Move infrequently accessed blobs to Cool or Archive tier
tips.description: Azure Blob Storage offers Hot, Cool, Cold, and Archive tiers. Move data accessed less than once per month to Cool (50% cheaper) or Archive (90% cheaper) with lifecycle management policies.

tips.title: Delete unattached Managed Disks
tips.description: Orphaned managed disks (not attached to any VM) continue incurring charges. Identify and delete unused disks or snapshot them to a cheaper tier before deletion.

tips.title: Right-size Azure SQL databases
tips.description: Scale down DTU or vCore tiers for databases with consistently low CPU and IO utilization. Use elastic pools to share resources across multiple databases with variable usage patterns.

tips.title: Use Azure SQL Reserved Capacity
tips.description: Reserve vCore compute capacity for Azure SQL Database or Managed Instance for 1 or 3 years. Provides significant discounts over pay-as-you-go pricing for predictable database workloads.

tips.title: Right-size AKS node pools and enable cluster autoscaler
tips.description: Enable AKS cluster autoscaler to scale node pools based on pod demand. Use Virtual Nodes for burst workloads and configure resource requests/limits to improve bin-packing efficiency.

tips.title: Delete unused Public IP addresses and idle Load Balancers
tips.description: Unassociated Public IP addresses and idle Standard Load Balancers incur hourly charges. Remove unused networking resources identified by Azure Advisor.

tips.title: Scale down idle App Service plans
tips.description: Downgrade underutilized App Service plans from Premium to Standard or Basic tier. Consider consolidating multiple low-traffic apps into a single plan.

tips.title: Optimize Log Analytics data ingestion
tips.description: Reduce Log Analytics costs by filtering verbose logs at collection, setting appropriate retention periods, using Basic Logs tier for debug data, and archiving old data to cheaper storage.

tips.title: Enforce tagging policies for cost allocation
tips.description: Use Azure Policy to enforce mandatory tags (cost-center, environment, owner) on all resources. Tags enable accurate chargeback, showback, and identification of unowned resources.
